issue_title: systemd-cryptsetup: Support keys other than RSA to pkcs11 enrollment
issue_body:
### Component

systemd-cryptsetup

### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

Yubikey 5 supports ECC P-256 keys for the PIV certificates. I would like to be able to enroll that key with systemd-cryptenroll to unlock my luks volume.

### Describe the solution you'd like

[Checking the source](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/blob/main/src/cryptenroll/cryptenroll-pkcs11.c#L44-L46) I can see that the assumption is that all enrolled keys are RSA. There're quite a few alternatives out there now and HSMs are starting to support them. It'd be great if support for e.g. P256 and ed22519 were added.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

For now I'll either enroll an RSA key in a different slot or switch the PIV cert key to RSA.

### The systemd version you checked that didn't have the feature you are asking for

252
pr_title: cryptsetup: Add support for EC keys in PKCS#11 tokens
